title: Entrance system
abstract: Present invention relates to an entrance system (10) comprising a control arrangement (20) for controlling the automatic door operator (30). The entrance system (10) is configured to operate in any one of the following states: an opening state (OPS) wherein the one or more movable door members (D1â¦Dm) are moving between the closed position and the opened position towards the opened position; a closing state (CLS) wherein the one or more movable door members (D1â¦Dm) are moving between the opened position and the closed position towards the closed position. The control arrangement (20) is further configured to control the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the opening state (OPS) to the closing state (CLS) at a reversing position (RP) of the one or more door members (D1â¦Dm) variably disposed between the closed and opened position of said one or more movable door members (D1â¦Dm) without the entrance system (10) entering the opened state (OS). Present invention further relates to a control arrangement for an entrance system (10) and a method for operating said entrance system (10).
claims:
Claims 1. An entrance system (10) comprising: one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm); an automatic door operator (30) for causing movements of the one or more movable door members (Dl...Dm) between a closed position and an opened position corresponding to a closed state (CS) and an opened state (OS) of the entrance system (10), respectively; and a control arrangement (20) for controlling the automatic door operator (30); whereby the entrance system (10) is configured to operate in any one of the following states: an opening state (OPS) wherein the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are moving between the closed position and the opened position towards the opened position; and a closing state (CLS) wherein the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are moving between the opened position and the closed position towards the closed position; whereby the control arrangement (20) is further configured to control the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the opening state (OPS) to the closing state (CLS) at a reversing position (RP) of the one or more door members (Dl ...Dm) variably disposed between the closed and opened position of said one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) without the entrance system (10) entering the opened state (OS). 2. The entrance system (10) according to claim 1, whereby the control arrangement (30) comprises a controller (32) and a plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn), each sensor being connected to the controller (32) and being configured to monitor a respective zone (Zl ...Zn) at the entrance system (10) for presence or activity of at least one person or object, whereby at least one of said plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn) is configured to detect one or more properties associated with at least one person or object 

 about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn), and whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined at least partly based on the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10). 3. The entrance system (10) according to claim 1 or 2, whereby the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are configured to be held at the opened position in the opened state (OS) for allowing access through the entrance system (10) and the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are configured to be held at the closed position in the closed state (CS) for sealing the entrance system (10). 4. The entrance system (10) according to claim 2 or 3, whereby the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) include one or more of the following: the size, velocity, direction or shape of the person or object. 5. The entrance system (10) according to claim 4, whereby at least one sensor of the plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn) is configured to detect one or more properties associated with at least one person or object passing through the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn), whereby the controller (32) is configured to combine the properties associated with the at least one person or object passing through the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) and the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) and determine the reversing position (RP) based on said combination. 6. The entrance system (10) according to claim 5, whereby the properties associated with the at least one person or object passing through the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) include one or more of the following: the size, velocity, direction or shape of the person or object. 7. The entrance system (10) according to claim 4, whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined based on the velocity and direction of the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (ZI . .Zh). 8. The entrance system according to any one of the preceding claims, the entrance system being a sliding door system (410), the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) being one or more sliding door members, whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined as a distance (d) from the opened position. 9. The entrance system according to any one of the claims 1 to 7, the entrance being a swing door system (510), the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) being one or more swing door leafs, whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined as an angle (F) in an opening direction of the swing door leafs. 10. The entrance system according to claim 9, whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined at least partly based on the direction of the at least one person about to enter the entrance system (10) and the opening direction of the one or more swing door leaf in relation to the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn). 11. The entrance system (10) according to any one of claim 2 to 10, whereby the control arrangement (20) is configured to determine if a person or object entering the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) risks to collide with the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) in the closing state (CLS), whereby the control arrangement (20) is configured to control the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the closing state (CLS) to the opening state (OPS) in response to determining said risk. 12. The entrance system (10) according to any one of claim 2 to 11, whereby the control arrangement (20) is configured to determine a closing speed of the one or 

 more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) in the closing state (CLS) and cause said one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) to close at said closing speed in the closing state (CLS), whereby said closing speed is at least partly based on the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn). 13. The entrance system (10) according to any one of claim 2 to 12, whereby the control arrangement (20) is configured to determine an opening speed of the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) in the opening state (OPS) and cause said one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) to open at said opening speed in the opening state (OPS), whereby said opening speed is at least partly based on the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn). 14. The entrance system (10) according to any one of claim 2 to 13, whereby the control arrangement (20) is configured to cause entrance system (10) to enter the opening state (OPS) in response to detection of the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn). 15. A control arrangement (20) for an entrance system (10) having one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) and an automatic door operator (30) for causing movements of the one ore movable door members between a closed position and an opened position corresponding to a closed state (CS) and an opened state (OS) of the entrance system (10), respectively, the control arrangement (20) comprising: a controller (32); and a plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn), each sensor being connected to the controller (32) and being configured to monitor a respective zone (Zl ...Zn) at the entrance system (10) for presence or activity of at least one person or object; whereby whereby the entrance system (10) is configured to operate in any of the following states: 

 an opening state (OPS) wherein the one ore movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are moving between the closed position and the opened position towards the opened position; and a closing state (CLS) wherein the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) are moving between the opened position and the closed position towards the closed position; whereby at least one of said plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn) is configured to detect properties associated with at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through respective zone (Zl ...Zn); whereby the control arrangement (20) is further configured to control the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the opening state (OPS) to the closing state (CLS) at a reversing position (RP) of the one or more door members (Dl ...Dm) variably disposed between the closed and opened position of said one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) without the entrance system (10) entering the opened state (OS), whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined at least partly based on the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10). 16. A method (100) of operating an entrance system (10) having one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) and an automatic door operator (30) for causing movements of the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) between a closed position and an opened position corresponding to a closed state (CS) and an opened state (OS) of the entrance system (10), respectively, whereby the entrance system (10) comprises a control arrangement (20) for controlling the automatic door operator (30), the method comprising: controlling (130) the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the opening state (OPS) to the closing state (CLS) at a reversing position (RP) of the one or more door members (Dl ...Dm) variably disposed between the closed and opened position of said one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) without the entrance system (10) entering the opened state (OS). 17. The method according to claim 16, whereby the control arrangement (20) comprising a controller (32) and a plurality of sensors (Sl ...Sn) each sensor being connected to the controller (32) and being configured to monitor a respective zone (Zl ...Zn) at the entrance system (10) for presence or activity of at least one person or object, the method further comprising: detecting (110) one or more properties associated with at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn); and determining (120) the reversing position (RP) at least partly based on the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10). 18. The method according to claim 17, whereby the properties associated with the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) include one or more of the following: the size, velocity, direction or shape of the person or object. 19. The method according to claim 17 or 18, whereby the method further comprises: detecting one or more properties associated with at least one person or object passing through the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn); combining the properties associated with the at least one person or object passing though the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) and the properties associated with the person or object about enter the entrance system, through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn); and determining the reversing position (RP) based on said combination. 20. The method according to claim 19, whereby the properties associated with the at least one person or object passing through the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) include one or more of the following: the size, velocity, direction or shape of the person or object. 21. The method according to claim 18, whereby the reversing position (RP) is determined based on the velocity and direction of the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10). 22. The method according to any one of claim 17 to 21, whereby the method further comprises: determining if a person or object entering the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) risks to collide with the one or more movable door members (Dl ...Dm) in the closing state (CLS); controlling the automatic door operator (30) so as to cause the entrance system (10) to switch from the closing state (CLS) to the opening state (OPS) in response to a determined risk for collision. 23. The method according to any of claim 17 to 22, whereby the method further comprises: detecting at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn) causing the entrance system (10) to enter the opening state (OPS) in response to detection of the at least one person or object about to enter the entrance system (10) through the respective zone (Zl ...Zn).
